title: Tests — doctor side (L1 + L2 drift detection + marker migration + ledger event)
description: Add service-layer drift detection + marker migration tests in doctor.test.ts. Three test groups: (1) L1 bootstrap_snapshot_drift detection + --fix overwrite + idempotency, (2) L2 managed_block_drift detection across three-end targets + --fix overwrite + idempotency, (3) marker migration from legacy fabric:knowledge-base markers + bootstrap_marker_migrated ledger event assertion. Mirrors existing bootstrap_anchor_missing triplet (L737-L795) + mcp_config_migrated --fix + ledger event pattern (L202-L286). Mandatory FABRIC_HOME isolation beforeEach.

files.change: Add `describe('rc.19 L1 bootstrap snapshot drift', () => { ... })`. Three tests: (a) 'reports ok when .fabric/AGENTS.md byte-equals BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL' — create project, install canonical, runDoctorReport, assert status='ok' + no 'bootstrap_snapshot_drift' in fixable_errors. (b) 'reports fixable_error when .fabric/AGENTS.md bytes differ' — same setup, mutate snapshot by one char, runDoctorReport, assert code='bootstrap_snapshot_drift' in fixable_errors. (c) '--fix restores byte-equality and second --fix is a no-op' — continue (b), runDoctorFix, assert .fabric/AGENTS.md byte-equals BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL, re-runDoctorFix returns changed=false. Use FABRIC_HOME isolation beforeEach pattern from L20-L40.

Add `describe('rc.19 L2 managed block drift', () => { ... })`. Three tests per managed target (AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc / CLAUDE.md @-import): (a) 'reports ok when all three managed blocks byte-equal expected concat', (b) 'reports drift when AGENTS.md block bytes differ', (c) '--fix rewrites all three blocks and idempotent on re-run'. Also include test '@.fabric/AGENTS.md line missing from CLAUDE.md triggers L2 drift' (special case for Claude thin shell). Include CRLF drift test: rewrite .fabric/AGENTS.md with \r\n line endings → assert L1 drift fires (NO normalization guard).

Add `describe('rc.19 marker migration fabric:knowledge-base → fabric:bootstrap', () => { ... })`. Four tests: (a) 'reports fixable_error when CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules contain legacy fabric:knowledge-base markers' — seed legacy markers via writeFixtureFile, runDoctorReport, assert code='bootstrap_marker_migration_required'. (b) '--fix rewrites legacy markers to fabric:bootstrap in all four target paths' — runDoctorFix, assert each file: zero fabric:knowledge-base substrings + exactly one fabric:bootstrap:begin + exactly one fabric:bootstrap:end. (c) '--fix emits bootstrap_marker_migrated ledger event per file migrated' — read events.jsonl post-fix, parse JSON lines, assert events.filter(e => e.event_type === 'bootstrap_marker_migrated').length === migrated file count. Use existing mcp_config_migrated assertion pattern as reference. (d) 'idempotent: re-running --fix after migration is a no-op' — runDoctorFix twice, second invocation produces zero new events.

files.target: fixable_errors code array assertions (existing tests, e.g. L55-L62)
files.change: Existing tests that assert exact fixable_errors[].map(i => i.code) arrays may need updates if rc.19's three new codes (bootstrap_snapshot_drift, managed_block_drift, bootstrap_marker_migration_required) appear in their fixtures. Audit each existing fixable_errors assertion; expand arrays accordingly OR adjust fixtures so the new codes don't fire in unrelated tests.

Verify the project setup helpers correctly install .fabric/AGENTS.md with BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL (post-rc.19 install state). If they bypass install and just write minimal files, extend the helper to also write .fabric/AGENTS.md so L1 drift tests have a baseline. Likely additive only.

implementation: Inspect existing doctor.test.ts setup (createProject / createInitializedProject helpers; FABRIC_HOME isolation pattern L20-L40)

Add L1 bootstrap_snapshot_drift describe block with 3 tests using the existing fixture builder + seedDriftedFile pattern adapted to server-side (file mutation via fs.writeFileSync, since seedDriftedFile lives in CLI test helpers)
Add L2 managed_block_drift describe block with 3 tests (one per managed target + CLAUDE.md @-import edge case + CRLF drift regression guard)
Add marker migration describe block with 4 tests (detection / fix / ledger event / idempotency)
Audit existing fixable_errors[].map assertions across the file — extend arrays where new codes legitimately appear in those test fixtures
Verify createProject helper installs .fabric/AGENTS.md with canonical content (extend if needed)
Run `pnpm --filter=@fenglimg/fabric-server test` and verify all new tests pass
Verify FABRIC_HOME isolation in every new test (beforeEach mkdtempSync + process.env.FABRIC_HOME = fakeHome)

reference.pattern: bootstrap_anchor_missing test triplet (doctor.test.ts:737-795) — three-test shape for inspect → check → fix. mcp_config_in_wrong_file --fix + ledger event (doctor.test.ts:202-286) — events.jsonl assertion via readFileSync + JSON.parse per line + filter by event_type.
reference.examples: FABRIC_HOME isolation beforeEach (L20-L29) is MANDATORY. fixable_errors[].map(i => i.code) assertion pattern (L55-L62) — extend for new codes.

criteria: Three new describe blocks added: 'rc.19 L1 bootstrap snapshot drift', 'rc.19 L2 managed block drift', 'rc.19 marker migration fabric:knowledge-base → fabric:bootstrap'
Minimum 10 new tests across the three blocks (3 + 4 + 4 = 11 minimum)
All new tests use FABRIC_HOME isolation beforeEach (verified by grep for process.env.FABRIC_HOME = within each describe)
CRLF drift regression test exists and asserts drift fires when .fabric/AGENTS.md has \r\n line endings (no normalization invariant guard)
bootstrap_marker_migrated ledger event test reads .fabric/events.jsonl post-fix and asserts via filter(e => e.event_type === 'bootstrap_marker_migrated').length ≥ 1
Idempotency test for each --fix path: second runDoctorFix call produces zero new file mutations and zero new ledger events
All existing tests in doctor.test.ts continue to pass (no regression in pre-rc.19 assertions)

rationale.chosen_approach: Service-layer tests in doctor.test.ts (real runDoctorReport / runDoctorFix logic) — per exploration test_concerns 'Mocked-server tests don't catch real server-side regressions' (high severity). Skip CLI-layer doctor tests per recent rc.17 pattern.
alternatives_considered: Add CLI-layer mocked tests in packages/cli/__tests__/doctor.test.ts (loses real service coverage)
Split L1 + L2 + migration tests into separate files (over-engineering for ≤ 15 tests total)
decision_factors: Single test file matches existing convention
rationale.tradeoffs: No CLI flag plumbing coverage. Acceptable; rc.17 doctor.test.ts pattern set the precedent and flag plumbing is unchanged in rc.19.

manual_checks: Verify zero references to fabric:knowledge-base outside the marker migration test (which deliberately seeds the legacy state)
success_metrics: All new tests pass on first run
Existing tests unchanged or extended (no regressions)
FABRIC_HOME isolation present in every new test

risks.description: FABRIC_HOME isolation forgotten in new tests pollutes developer's real ~/.fabric
risks.mitigation: Code review checklist: every new doctor test must include the FABRIC_HOME beforeEach block (L20-L29 pattern). Lint/grep can verify.

risks.description: createProject helper produces a baseline that conflicts with new L1/L2 drift fixtures
risks.mitigation: Inspect createProject helper before writing tests; if it pre-installs .fabric/AGENTS.md with canonical content, drift tests will need to mutate AFTER setup. Adjust accordingly.

risks.description: Existing fixable_errors[].map assertions may break when new codes are introduced into shared fixtures
risks.mitigation: Run the test suite immediately after wiring TASK-04 + TASK-05 into runDoctorReport; fix any existing-test failures by expanding the asserted code arrays or adjusting fixtures so new codes don't fire in those tests
